---
title: "Resume"
toc: false
---

::: {style="display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: baseline; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 1rem; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;"}
::: {style="flex: 1; min-width: 280px;"}
# Jesse G

**Manager of Analytics Insights · TV Attribution & Media Measurement**
:::

::: {style="text-align: right; font-size: 0.92rem; color: var(--ink-soft);"}
[idgesus@gmail.com](mailto:idgesus@gmail.com)
[LinkedIn](https://linkedin.com/in/YOURUSERNAME) · [GitHub](https://github.com/YOURUSERNAME)
:::
:::

::: {style="border-top: 2px solid var(--ink); margin: 1rem 0 2rem;"}
:::

[
::: {.featured-card style="background: var(--accent-soft); border-color: rgba(37,99,235,0.2);"}
**📄 Download PDF version** — *Generate a PDF from this page once you have the assets ready (or use a tailored design service like FlowCV).*
:::
](#)


## Summary

Analytics leader with **13+ years** building measurement frameworks that connect media investment to business outcomes. Specialized in TV attribution, probabilistic modeling, spike detection, and code-driven reporting infrastructure. Currently lead a team of analysts at JuiceMedia, having grown into the role through Senior Analyst, Manager, and Director progression.

Known for combining **statistical rigor** with **operational discipline** — the rare analytics leader who can architect a Bayesian model and the AWS-backed pipeline that delivers it to a CMO every Monday morning.

---

## Experience

### Manager of Analytics Insights
**JuiceMedia** · *2020 – Present*

- Lead the Analytics Insights team measuring TV and multi-channel campaigns for clients across pharma, telemarketing, and media verticals
- Architect probabilistic attribution and DMA-level lift measurement frameworks used in weekly client deliverables
- Built the automated reporting infrastructure (Quarto, gt tables, AWS S3 + Athena, Excel/openxlsx) that **eliminated 8+ hours/week of manual analyst work**
- Designed spike detection systems for real-time identification of media-driven traffic events
- Manage **3+ analysts**: methodology standards, technical reviews, mentorship, hiring partnership
- Partner cross-functionally with engineering, data science, and client success teams to ship integrated analytics products
- Present findings and methodology to client VP/C-level stakeholders

### Senior Analyst → Manager → Director
**DirectAvenue / JuiceMedia** · *2016 – 2020*

- Promoted through three levels while building the measurement practice
- Owned methodology for DMA-level TV attribution and lift analysis
- Designed the spike detection framework still used across the company
- Established executive reporting cadence and templates

### Data Analyst → Senior Analyst
**Encore Capital Group** · *2012 – 2016*

- Built reporting and analytical systems supporting data-driven decision making in financial services
- Developed statistical models and quantitative analyses
- Created automated reporting workflows and executive presentations

---

## Education

**B.A. Economics (Quantitative Analysis) & Statistics**
San Diego State University · *2012*

Double major combining economic theory with applied statistical methods.

---

## Technical Skills

**Languages & Tools:** Python, R, SQL, Quarto, Excel/VBA
**Data engineering:** Parquet, Polars, DuckDB, pyarrow, AWS (S3, Athena), Hive partitioning
**Statistics:** Causal inference, synthetic controls, probabilistic attribution, Bayesian methods (familiar), time-series analysis, anomaly detection, A/B & geo-testing
**Reporting & viz:** Quarto, gt/gtExtras, ggplot2, Plotly, Tableau, openxlsx2, matplotlib
**Engineering practices:** Git, GitHub, code review, testing, reproducible pipelines, parameterized reports

---

## Domain Expertise

- **TV attribution & lift analysis** at DMA and national level
- **Probabilistic attribution modeling** across overlapping media channels
- **Spike detection** and anomaly detection on time-series traffic data
- **Automated executive reporting** — Quarto/gt/Excel pipeline design
- **Pharma, telemarketing, and media** vertical experience
- **Team leadership** — managing analysts, defining standards, mentorship

---

## Featured Writing

[**The Case for Code-Driven Reporting: Why Your Analytics Team Should Stop Building Decks**](posts/code-driven-reporting.html) — A methodology paper on treating analytical reports as software products. *2025.*

---

::: {style="text-align: center; font-size: 0.85rem; color: var(--ink-muted); margin-top: 3rem;"}
For case studies and code, see [the portfolio →](index.html)
:::
